behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module handoff (manifest validation must gate module import/execution), not just pure schema logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess/OS primitive beyond normal local file/module loading, so no System requirement. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked importer could hide accidental early import, so wire real loader+validator+import path with fixture plugins. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Slot A: "rejected before any code from that plugin is executed." Slot B: handoff (validation -> import gate). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can pass validation logic tests while still importing the plugin module too early. Final label: Integration because Slot B is a cross-module gate and Slot C is missed by Unit. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/OS event semantics; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid manifest and an entry module that writes a sentinel file at import time. Run the real loader. Assert the plugin is reported rejected and the sentinel file was never created (proves no module code executed).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires real module wiring and import gating, which is above Unit but below System.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is dependency-resolution handoff plus lifecycle ordering across modules/plugins. REAL-INFRA: no need for subprocess/real network/OS-only behavior. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only topo-sort can miss miswiring between resolved order and actual hook invocation order. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Slot A: "plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's." Slot B: handoff (resolver output -> hook executor). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, sort function may be correct while executor still calls hooks in discovery order. Final label: Integration due to Slot B and missed Slot C failure. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because required failure is ordering in in-process orchestration, not OS/network/subprocess behavior; Agentic/Workflow rejected by absence of model-driven decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real fixture plugins A and B on disk with B dependency declared by A. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared log. Run loader and assert log order is `["B","A"]`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must exercise resolver+executor integration; a lower-level unit test would not reliably catch wiring defects.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior combines cycle detection with enforcement that neither plugin loads (detector -> loader gate). REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network/OS event dependency needed. MOCK-HIDE: isolated graph-unit tests can pass while loader still partially initializes one plugin after cycle error. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Slot A: "reports the cycle and loads neither." Slot B: handoff (cycle result -> load suppression). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, algorithm may detect cycle but runtime may still invoke one `onLoad`. Final label: Integration because suppression behavior is cross-module orchestration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected since no real external infra behavior is the failure boundary; Agentic/Workflow rejected since no AI output dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependency and `onLoad` side effects writing to a log. Run real loader, assert cycle error is emitted, and assert log is empty (no plugin loaded).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs detector + loader interaction; pure unit cycle check is insufficient.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is ordering across import subsystem and lifecycle runner. REAL-INFRA: uses real Node module import semantics, but still in-process/local filesystem; no subprocess/network/OS primitive required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking import can mask true execution timing/side effects and pass even when real import order is wrong. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Slot A: "`onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed." Slot B: handoff (module execution completion -> hook invocation). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a fake importer may immediately resolve and miss race/order bugs with real module side effects. Final label: Integration because true failure sits at module-boundary ordering. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no subprocess/network/OS-only behavior, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System considered but rejected since behavior text does not require external process/OS-event/network conditions; Agentic/Workflow rejected by no model output involvement.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixture plugin entry module that sets `globalThis.pluginReady = true` on import, and `onLoad` that asserts this flag is already true and records execution. Run loader and assert no assertion failure plus observed sequence `import-side-effect` then `onLoad`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real import+hook wiring; unit-level stubs can hide ordering defects.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is error isolation plus dependency-aware continuation across loader components. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess/OS boundary needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests of try/catch logic can miss graph-aware skip/continue behavior in full initialization flow. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Slot A: "continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it." Slot B: handoff (hook failure -> scheduler/graph continuation policy). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you might verify exception handling but miss incorrect stop-the-world behavior or loading of forbidden dependents. Final label: Integration due to cross-module continuation semantics. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because no external infra condition defines correctness; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI model decisions are part of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: `X` throws in `onLoad`; `Y` independent; `Z` depends on `X`. Run loader and assert `Y` initializes, `Z` does not, and error for `X` is reported without aborting whole process.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior depends on orchestrated interactions among dependency graph, hook runner, and error handling.
